text: The Inter-Parliamentary Union estimates that there were about 5,500 women in parliament worldwide in spring 2002, representing 14.3% of all members, up from 9% in 1987. If growth at this level is maintained (0.36% per annum), a simple linear projection predicts that women parliamentarians will achieve parity with men at the turn of the twenty-second century.
author: Pippa Norris
source: Rising Tide: Gender Equality and Cultural Change around the World, Pippa Norris and Ronald Inglehart (Cambridge University Press, 2003), Chapter 6, p. 130
